name: South: Malabar Fish Poached in Coconut Milk
recipeIngredient: Red snapper (4 filets, about 5-1/2 ounces each)
Sole (or whitefish) (4 filets, about 5-1/2 ounces each)
Dried curry leaves (or 8 fresh curry leaves, minced cilantro) (2 tablespoons)
Fresh serrano chiles (1 or 2, stemmed and seeded)
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the fish: Rub fish filets with lemon juice, salt, and pepper. Let marinate for at least 30 minutes.
2. Cook the fish: Heat oil in a frying pan over medium-high heat. Add fish and cook until opaque, about 2 minutes per side. Remove with a slotted spoon.
3. Prepare the sauce: In the same pan, add remaining oil. Add curry leaves, onion, ginger, chiles, and turmeric. Cook until onions are soft (about 3 minutes).
4. Finish cooking: Add cumin, coconut, and coconut milk. Cook uncovered until slightly thickened (8 to 10 minutes). Gently return fish filets to the pan and cook for an additional minute.
5. Serve: Transfer fish with sauce to a heated platter and serve.
